citations.narrative:
There was a mild accumulation of debris present on the shelves of the refrigerator where fresh produce is stored for the
farm animals. Additionally, there was a moderate accumulation of dust and debris on the top and the sides of the
refrigerator. This may pose a risk of food spoilage and contamination and should be cleaned. Supplies of food shall be
stored in facilities which adequately protect such supplies against deterioration, molding, and contamination.
To be corrected by February 18, 2026

There is a section of wall space, approximately 2 feet X 1 foot in the barn/ shelter space of the farm animal enclosure that
is stained from excreta with residual debris present. Although the barn is on a regular cleaning schedule, walls should
also be included and maintained as required to reduce disease hazards in animal enclosures. Excreta shall be removed
from primary enclosures as often as necessary to prevent contamination of animals contained therein and to minimize
disease hazards and to reduce odors.
To be corrected by February 18, 2026
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the facility representative.
n

narrative:
3.125(c) 
   
Facilities, general. 
There was a mild accumulation of debris present on the shelves of the refrigerator where fresh produce is stored for the 
farm animals. Additionally, there was a moderate accumulation of dust and debris on the top and the sides of the 
refrigerator. This may pose a risk of food spoilage and contamination and should be cleaned. Supplies of food shall be 
stored in facilities which adequately protect such supplies against deterioration, molding, and contamination. 
To be corrected by February 18, 2026 
3.131(a) 
   
Sanitation. 
There is a section of wall space, approximately 2 feet X 1 foot in the barn/ shelter space of the farm animal enclosure that 
is stained from excreta with residual debris present. Although the barn is on a regular cleaning schedule, walls should 
also be included and maintained as required to reduce disease hazards in animal enclosures. Excreta shall be removed 
from primary enclosures as often as necessary to prevent contamination of animals contained therein and to minimize 
disease hazards and to reduce odors.  
To be corrected by February 18, 2026 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the facility representative.
